sections.paragraph:
Ζεύς, Διός, acc. Δία (Hom. et al.; ins; 2 Macc 6:2; EpArist 16; Philo, Joseph., SibOr, apolog. exc. Mel.) Zeus, king of the Greek gods, thought by the people of Lystra to have appeared in the pers. of Barnabas Ac 14:12. His role as benefactor to those engaged in agricultural pursuits (cp. Hymn to Zeus Diktaios in AnthLG, Diehl2 II, 279–81) prob. serves as a motivational component that accounts for the counter-description of ‘the living God’ (vss. 15–17) and the extraordinary response of the audience (vs. 18). ὁ ἱερεὺς τ. Διὸς τ. ὄντος πρὸ τ. πόλεως the priest of (the temple/shrine of) Zeus located before the city vs. 13 (cp. Chariton 4, 4, 9 εἰς Ἀφροδίτην βαδίζειν; Philostrat. I 363, 17 ἐν Ἀρτέμιδι=in the temple of Artemis; CBreytenbach, NTS 39, ’93, 396–413 [ins.]).—Lit. LfgrE 860 s.v. Ζεύς; also SEG XLIII, 1311. 1758–71. S. entry Λύστρα.

acc. acc. = accusative

Hom Hom , VIII b.c.—List 5

et al. et al. = et alii (and others)

ins Ins, ins = Inscription, Inschrift, inscription(s). Without a period, esp. in lists, as at the beginning of entries; the capitalized form is used in titles. In conjunction with literary works this abbr. refers to the title or description of contents.

EpArist EpArist = Epistle of Aristeas, II b.c.—List 5

Philo Philo = P. of Alexandria, I b.c.–I a.d.—List 5

Joseph Joseph This abbr. used when no specific texts are cited (s. Jos.); I a.d.—List 5

SibOr SibOr = Oracula Sibyllina, II–III a.d.—List 5

apolog. apolog. = apologist(s)

exc. exc. = except

Mel. Mel. = Melito of Sardis, II a.d.—List 5

pers. pers. = person(s)

  1. cp. = compare, freq. in ref. to citation fr. ancient texts

AnthLG AnthLG = Anthologia Lyrica Graeca—List 5

Diehl Diehl , ed., s. AnthLG—List 5

prob. prob. = probable, probably

Chariton Chariton , scriptor eroticus (writer of romances) I–II a.d.—List 5

Philostrat Philostrat = Flavius Philostratus, the Sophist II–III a.d.—List 5

NTS NTS = New Testament Studies—List 6

LfgrE LfgrE = Lexicon des frühgriechischen Epos—List 6

s.v. s.v. = sub voce (under the word, look up the word)

SEG SEG = Supplementum Epigraphicum Graecum—List 3

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 426
